description_long: This is a vintage wall-mounted corded telephone, primarily constructed from polished wood with brass and metal accents. The front features a prominent rotary dial with visible numbers and a metallic finger stop, flanked by two brass bells above for the ringer. A wooden and brass handset is attached to the left side of the unit, connected by a coiled black cord, and rests on a dedicated cradle. The lower section of the phone has a curved wooden shelf, likely for writing or holding small items. The back and sides are smooth, showcasing the wood grain, with minimal protrusions except for the handset and cradle. The overall design is ornate and classic, reminiscent of early 20th-century telephones often found in private homes or historical settings.
description: A vintage wall-mounted wooden corded phone with a rotary dial, brass bells, and a coiled corded handset resting on a side cradle. The design is ornate, with polished wood and metallic accents.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rotary dial, brass bells, coiled cord, and handset on the left cradle.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the handset profile, cradle, and the depth of the wooden shelf.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the smooth wooden back panel with no visible features except the handset cord.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the brass bell protrusion and the handset's rear profile.
